reason: Main target: the independent-review step requires the claude-only `Agent` tool (subagent spawn) and references ~/.claude/skills/decide-direction/references/direction-critic.md. Add an explicit degraded mode for runtimes without a subagent mechanism and make the reference path dual (~/.claude/skills/... or ~/.agents/skills/..., whichever exists).
Style reference ONLY (do not edit): its 'No subagent mechanism available → degraded mode' wording is the pattern to follow — states when degraded applies and what the cost is.
Style reference ONLY (do not edit): already dual-path (.agents/ or .claude/) — the path-reference pattern to follow.

R3a: the role.yaml template hardcodes `provider: claude-code` (~line 120); change to a follow-the-fleet instruction — draft with the company's current majority provider from existing agents/*.yaml, stated explicitly.

R3b: line ~283 `data.get("provider", "claude-code")` — introduce fleet_default_provider(agents_dir) (majority provider across existing role yamls; tie/empty → claude-code) as the single source for the absent-provider resolution. ALLOWED_PROVIDERS whitelist semantics unchanged.
R3b: when materializing a proposal whose role.yaml omits provider, write the fleet default EXPLICITLY into the produced agents/<name>.yaml (no implicit default on disk).
Tests for fleet_default_provider (all-codex fleet → codex; mixed tie → claude-code) and lint behavior with absent provider.
Test: proposal without provider materializes an agents/<name>.yaml with explicit provider = fleet majority.
